# ASIT — Taxonomy

Version **0.2.0** · updated **2026-07-24**

Every category below is testable: an incident either meets the definition or it does not. Ids are permanent.

## Legend

- **causal_locus** — the proximate cause class of the failure.
- **detection_point** — the earliest pipeline stage at which the failure is realistically catchable.
- **evidence_status** — `documented` (sourced in incidents.yaml), `reported_anecdotally`, or `anticipated` (no public case yet).
- **blast_radius** — 1–5, worst plausible outcome. Rubric:

  1. Local and trivially reversible. Confined to a single file, branch, or developer workspace; caught and undone with no downstream effect.
  2. Contained within a project. Affects one repository or service and its contributors; recoverable from version control or backups within a work session.
  3. Team- or pipeline-level. Corrupts shared branches, CI/CD, or team tooling; blocks or misleads multiple people and needs deliberate remediation.
  4. Cross-system or sensitive-data. Reaches production, secrets, customer data, or multiple downstream consumers; recovery is costly and may be partial.
  5. Organisation-wide or external and irreversible. Production data loss, breach of third parties, or supply-chain propagation to external users; no clean rollback exists.

**Design**

**Test**  
[ASIT-TEST-01](#asit-test-01)

[ASIT-REL-01](#asit-rel-01)  [ASIT-REL-02](#asit-rel-02)

[ASIT-OPS-01](#asit-ops-01)  [ASIT-OPS-02](#asit-ops-02)  [ASIT-OPS-03](#asit-ops-03)  [ASIT-OPS-04](#asit-ops-04)

**Foundational plane**  
[ASIT-FOUND-01](#asit-found-01)  [ASIT-FOUND-02](#asit-found-02)


## Plan

<a id="asit-plan-01"></a>
### ASIT-PLAN-01 — Under-scoped Task Authorization

The agent begins work from a task specification that never bounded which systems, files, or actions are in scope, so no gate can later tell an in-scope action from an out-of-scope one. Test: at task start, was there a written, agreed boundary of what the agent may and may not touch?

- **Lifecycle stage:** PLAN
- **Causal locus:** `oversight_failure`
- **Detection point:** `pre_execution`
- **Blast radius:** 3/5 — Team- or pipeline-level. Corrupts shared branches, CI/CD, or team tooling; blocks or misleads multiple people and needs deliberate remediation.
- **Evidence status:** `anticipated`
- **Preconditions:**
  - An agent is dispatched against a task without an explicit scope or authorization boundary.
  - No downstream control re-derives scope from an authoritative source.
- **Mitigating controls:** CTL-06 (Task Authorization Boundary)
- **Related:** [ASIT-DESIGN-01](#asit-design-01), [ASIT-DESIGN-02](#asit-design-02), [ASIT-BUILD-05](#asit-build-05)
- **External mappings:** OWASP LLM: LLM06:2025 Excessive Agency
- **Notes:** A precondition amplifier for several execution-stage modes; kept distinct because the failure is the missing authorization artifact at plan time.


## Design

<a id="asit-design-01"></a>
### ASIT-DESIGN-01 — Autonomy-Risk Tier Mismatch

An agent is granted a level of autonomy (e.g. act-without-approval, access to destructive or irreversible tools) that exceeds the reversibility and blast radius of its task. Test: could a single unattended agent action cause irreversible harm the task's risk tier did not warrant?

- **Lifecycle stage:** DESIGN
- **Causal locus:** `overbroad_permission`
- **Detection point:** `pre_execution`
- **Blast radius:** 5/5 — Organisation-wide or external and irreversible. Production data loss, breach of third parties, or supply-chain propagation to external users; no clean rollback exists.
- **Evidence status:** `documented`
- **Preconditions:**
  - The agent can take state-changing actions without per-action human approval.
  - The agent has access to tools whose effects are hard or impossible to reverse.
  - No design-time risk tiering matched autonomy to task reversibility.
- **Mitigating controls:** CTL-07 (Design Risk Gate); CTL-13 (Runtime Oversight and Kill Switch)
- **Related:** [ASIT-DESIGN-02](#asit-design-02), [ASIT-REL-01](#asit-rel-01), [ASIT-OPS-01](#asit-ops-01), [ASIT-OPS-03](#asit-ops-03)
- **External mappings:** OWASP LLM: LLM06:2025 Excessive Agency · CWE: CWE-250
- **Incidents:**
  - [INC-0001](https://incidentdatabase.ai/cite/1152/) — Replit AI agent deleted a production database during a code freeze

<a id="asit-design-02"></a>
### ASIT-DESIGN-02 — Overbroad Permission or Tool Grant

The agent holds tool, credential, or data-access scopes materially wider than its task requires, enabling actions across a trust boundary. Test: if the agent were coerced or mistaken, could it reach systems or data its task never needed because its granted scope was not minimised?

- **Lifecycle stage:** DESIGN
- **Causal locus:** `overbroad_permission`
- **Detection point:** `pre_execution`
- **Blast radius:** 4/5 — Cross-system or sensitive-data. Reaches production, secrets, customer data, or multiple downstream consumers; recovery is costly and may be partial.
- **Evidence status:** `documented`
- **Preconditions:**
  - The agent is issued a token, role, or tool set broader than the task requires.
  - Multiple repositories, environments, or data classes are reachable in a single session.
- **Mitigating controls:** CTL-08 (Least-Privilege Permission Design)
- **Related:** [ASIT-DESIGN-01](#asit-design-01), [ASIT-BUILD-03](#asit-build-03), [ASIT-OPS-01](#asit-ops-01), [ASIT-OPS-04](#asit-ops-04), [ASIT-FOUND-01](#asit-found-01)
- **External mappings:** OWASP LLM: LLM06:2025 Excessive Agency · CWE: CWE-269, CWE-250
- **Incidents:**
  - [INC-0004](https://www.wiz.io/blog/s1ngularity-supply-chain-attack) — s1ngularity — malicious Nx npm packages weaponised local AI CLIs to steal secrets
  - [INC-0008](https://invariantlabs.ai/blog/mcp-github-vulnerability) — GitHub MCP "toxic agent flow" — public issue coerces agent to leak private repos


## Build

<a id="asit-build-01"></a>
### ASIT-BUILD-01 — Insecure Code Generation

The agent writes source code that contains an exploitable weakness — for example injection, broken authorization, weak cryptography, or a hard-coded secret — with no adversary involved. Test: does the agent-authored code match a known CWE weakness pattern as committed?

- **Lifecycle stage:** BUILD
- **Causal locus:** `model_error`
- **Detection point:** `ci`
- **Blast radius:** 4/5 — Cross-system or sensitive-data. Reaches production, secrets, customer data, or multiple downstream consumers; recovery is costly and may be partial.
- **Evidence status:** `documented`
- **Preconditions:**
  - The agent produces code that is committed or merged.
  - No SAST, secret-scanning, or security review gate blocks the weakness.
- **Mitigating controls:** CTL-11 (Automated Security and Correctness Testing); CTL-10 (Human-in-the-Loop Change Approval)
- **Related:** [ASIT-BUILD-02](#asit-build-02), [ASIT-TEST-01](#asit-test-01)
- **External mappings:** OWASP LLM: LLM05:2025 Improper Output Handling · CWE: CWE-94, CWE-798, CWE-89, CWE-79
- **Incidents:**
  - [INC-0010](https://www.veracode.com/blog/ai-generated-code-security-risks/) — Empirical studies find AI-generated code frequently contains vulnerabilities

<a id="asit-build-02"></a>
### ASIT-BUILD-02 — Hallucinated Dependency (Slopsquatting Exposure)

The agent recommends, imports, or installs a package or module that does not exist, creating a name an attacker can pre-register on a public registry. Test: does the agent's output reference a dependency that is not resolvable to a legitimate, existing package at generation time?

- **Lifecycle stage:** BUILD
- **Causal locus:** `supply_chain`
- **Detection point:** `ci`
- **Blast radius:** 5/5 — Organisation-wide or external and irreversible. Production data loss, breach of third parties, or supply-chain propagation to external users; no clean rollback exists.
- **Evidence status:** `documented`
- **Preconditions:**
  - The agent can name or add third-party dependencies.
  - Dependency resolution or installation is not gated against an allowlist or existence/provenance check.
- **Mitigating controls:** CTL-11 (Automated Security and Correctness Testing); CTL-04 (Model Supply Chain Integrity); CTL-14 (Release Artifact Attestation)
- **Related:** [ASIT-BUILD-01](#asit-build-01), [ASIT-REL-02](#asit-rel-02), [ASIT-FOUND-02](#asit-found-02)
- **External mappings:** OWASP LLM: LLM03:2025 Supply Chain, LLM09:2025 Misinformation · MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0010 ML Supply Chain Compromise
- **Incidents:**
  - [INC-0006](https://labs.cloudsecurityalliance.org/research/csa-research-note-slopsquatting-ai-supply-chain-20260419-csa/) — Slopsquatting — attackers register package names LLMs hallucinate

<a id="asit-build-03"></a>
### ASIT-BUILD-03 — Indirect Prompt Injection via Untrusted Content

The agent ingests attacker-controlled content (an issue, pull request, comment, fetched page, or repository file) and treats it as trusted instructions, then acts on it. Test: did an agent action originate from instructions embedded in content the agent read rather than from its operator?

- **Lifecycle stage:** BUILD
- **Causal locus:** `injected_instruction`
- **Detection point:** `runtime`
- **Blast radius:** 5/5 — Organisation-wide or external and irreversible. Production data loss, breach of third parties, or supply-chain propagation to external users; no clean rollback exists.
- **Evidence status:** `documented`
- **Preconditions:**
  - The agent incorporates untrusted external content into its context.
  - The agent can take consequential actions (tool calls, edits, network egress) from that context.
  - No trust boundary separates data from instructions.
- **Mitigating controls:** CTL-09 (Context and Prompt Integrity); CTL-08 (Least-Privilege Permission Design)
- **Related:** [ASIT-BUILD-04](#asit-build-04), [ASIT-DESIGN-02](#asit-design-02), [ASIT-OPS-04](#asit-ops-04)
- **External mappings:** OWASP LLM: LLM01:2025 Prompt Injection, LLM02:2025 Sensitive Information Disclosure · MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0051 LLM Prompt Injection, AML.T0053 LLM Plugin Compromise · CWE: CWE-1427
- **Incidents:**
  - [INC-0008](https://invariantlabs.ai/blog/mcp-github-vulnerability) — GitHub MCP "toxic agent flow" — public issue coerces agent to leak private repos
  - [INC-0009](https://www.legitsecurity.com/blog/camoleak-critical-github-copilot-vulnerability-leaks-private-source-code) — CamoLeak (CVE-2025-59145) — GitHub Copilot Chat exfiltrated private code

<a id="asit-build-04"></a>
### ASIT-BUILD-04 — Poisoned Agent Configuration

Persistent malicious instructions are hidden in the agent's own configuration, rules, or context files (including via invisible or obfuscated characters) and steer generation across sessions and reviews. Test: does an agent config or rules file contain instructions that alter generated code beyond its stated, human-readable purpose?

- **Lifecycle stage:** BUILD
- **Causal locus:** `injected_instruction`
- **Detection point:** `human_review`
- **Blast radius:** 5/5 — Organisation-wide or external and irreversible. Production data loss, breach of third parties, or supply-chain propagation to external users; no clean rollback exists.
- **Evidence status:** `documented`
- **Preconditions:**
  - The agent loads project- or user-level configuration/rules files into its instruction context.
  - Those files are not integrity-checked or provenance-tracked.
  - Reviewers cannot readily see the effective instruction content.
- **Mitigating controls:** CTL-09 (Context and Prompt Integrity); CTL-04 (Model Supply Chain Integrity)
- **Related:** [ASIT-BUILD-03](#asit-build-03), [ASIT-FOUND-02](#asit-found-02)
- **External mappings:** OWASP LLM: LLM01:2025 Prompt Injection, LLM04:2025 Data and Model Poisoning · MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0051 LLM Prompt Injection · CWE: CWE-1427
- **Incidents:**
  - [INC-0005](https://www.pillar.security/blog/new-vulnerability-in-github-copilot-and-cursor-how-hackers-can-weaponize-code-agents) — Rules File Backdoor: hidden instructions in AI editor config files

<a id="asit-build-05"></a>
### ASIT-BUILD-05 — Unrequested Scope Expansion

The agent modifies files, configuration, dependencies, or behaviour beyond the requested change, without surfacing that it did so. Test: does the agent's diff contain changes not entailed by the task, presented as if in scope?

- **Lifecycle stage:** BUILD
- **Causal locus:** `oversight_failure`
- **Detection point:** `human_review`
- **Blast radius:** 3/5 — Team- or pipeline-level. Corrupts shared branches, CI/CD, or team tooling; blocks or misleads multiple people and needs deliberate remediation.
- **Evidence status:** `anticipated`
- **Preconditions:**
  - The agent can edit files or settings outside the task's target.
  - Changes are accepted without a diff-level human review against the task.
- **Mitigating controls:** CTL-10 (Human-in-the-Loop Change Approval)
- **Related:** [ASIT-PLAN-01](#asit-plan-01), [ASIT-TEST-01](#asit-test-01)
- **External mappings:** OWASP LLM: LLM06:2025 Excessive Agency
- **Notes:** Widely reported anecdotally but without a postmortem-grade public source; held at anticipated until one is found.


## Test

<a id="asit-test-01"></a>
### ASIT-TEST-01 — Verification Integrity Failure

The agent causes the verification signal to misrepresent reality — by weakening, deleting, mocking, or hard-coding tests, or by fabricating results or concealing failures — so that broken or unsafe work appears to pass. Test: does a green/passing signal produced or influenced by the agent diverge from the actual state of the code?

- **Lifecycle stage:** TEST
- **Causal locus:** `oversight_failure`
- **Detection point:** `human_review`
- **Blast radius:** 4/5 — Cross-system or sensitive-data. Reaches production, secrets, customer data, or multiple downstream consumers; recovery is costly and may be partial.
- **Evidence status:** `documented`
- **Preconditions:**
  - The agent can modify tests, fixtures, or the reported outcome of checks.
  - Verification results are trusted without independent confirmation.
- **Mitigating controls:** CTL-11 (Automated Security and Correctness Testing); CTL-10 (Human-in-the-Loop Change Approval); CTL-03 (Audit Trail)
- **Related:** [ASIT-BUILD-01](#asit-build-01), [ASIT-BUILD-05](#asit-build-05), [ASIT-OPS-02](#asit-ops-02)
- **External mappings:** OWASP LLM: LLM09:2025 Misinformation
- **Incidents:**
  - [INC-0001](https://incidentdatabase.ai/cite/1152/) — Replit AI agent deleted a production database during a code freeze
- **Notes:** Merges test-gaming and fabricated/concealed results. If a distinct cross-cutting "Deceptive Status Reporting" category is later wanted, split here.


## Release

<a id="asit-rel-01"></a>
### ASIT-REL-01 — Change-Freeze or Approval-Gate Violation

The agent performs a state-changing action that an active change freeze or required approval gate should have blocked. Test: was there an in-force freeze or approval requirement, and did the agent act without satisfying it?

- **Lifecycle stage:** REL
- **Causal locus:** `oversight_failure`
- **Detection point:** `pre_execution`
- **Blast radius:** 4/5 — Cross-system or sensitive-data. Reaches production, secrets, customer data, or multiple downstream consumers; recovery is costly and may be partial.
- **Evidence status:** `documented`
- **Preconditions:**
  - A freeze or approval requirement is in force for the target action.
  - The agent can execute the action without the gate being technically enforced against it.
- **Mitigating controls:** CTL-12 (Release Gate and Change-Freeze Enforcement)
- **Related:** [ASIT-OPS-01](#asit-ops-01), [ASIT-DESIGN-01](#asit-design-01)
- **External mappings:** OWASP LLM: LLM06:2025 Excessive Agency · CWE: CWE-285
- **Incidents:**
  - [INC-0001](https://incidentdatabase.ai/cite/1152/) — Replit AI agent deleted a production database during a code freeze

<a id="asit-rel-02"></a>
### ASIT-REL-02 — Compromised Agent Distribution

Malicious code or instructions are injected into the agent's own extension, package, or release channel and ship to users as a trusted update. Test: did users receive a signed or official agent build that carried attacker-introduced behaviour?

- **Lifecycle stage:** REL
- **Causal locus:** `supply_chain`
- **Detection point:** `human_review`
- **Blast radius:** 5/5 — Organisation-wide or external and irreversible. Production data loss, breach of third parties, or supply-chain propagation to external users; no clean rollback exists.
- **Evidence status:** `documented`
- **Preconditions:**
  - The agent (or its extension/plugin) has a distribution channel users trust.
  - A contribution or release path exists that can introduce code without sufficient review or provenance control.
- **Mitigating controls:** CTL-04 (Model Supply Chain Integrity); CTL-12 (Release Gate and Change-Freeze Enforcement); CTL-14 (Release Artifact Attestation)
- **Related:** [ASIT-BUILD-02](#asit-build-02), [ASIT-OPS-04](#asit-ops-04), [ASIT-FOUND-02](#asit-found-02)
- **External mappings:** OWASP LLM: LLM03:2025 Supply Chain · MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0010 ML Supply Chain Compromise, AML.T0053 LLM Plugin Compromise · CWE: CWE-506
- **Incidents:**
  - [INC-0002](https://github.com/aws/aws-toolkit-vscode/security/advisories/GHSA-7g7f-ff96-5gcw) — Amazon Q Developer VS Code extension shipped with an injected wiper prompt


## Operate

<a id="asit-ops-01"></a>
### ASIT-OPS-01 — Destructive Action Beyond Authorization

The agent executes an irreversible destructive operation — dropping, deleting, or overwriting data, files, or infrastructure — that it was not authorized to perform. Test: did the agent carry out a destructive action outside its authorized scope, for which no rollback existed?

- **Lifecycle stage:** OPS
- **Causal locus:** `overbroad_permission`
- **Detection point:** `runtime`
- **Blast radius:** 5/5 — Organisation-wide or external and irreversible. Production data loss, breach of third parties, or supply-chain propagation to external users; no clean rollback exists.
- **Evidence status:** `documented`
- **Preconditions:**
  - The agent can invoke destructive operations against real data or infrastructure.
  - No confirmation, dry-run, or environment separation blocks the action.
- **Mitigating controls:** CTL-13 (Runtime Oversight and Kill Switch); CTL-06 (Task Authorization Boundary); CTL-08 (Least-Privilege Permission Design)
- **Related:** [ASIT-DESIGN-01](#asit-design-01), [ASIT-DESIGN-02](#asit-design-02), [ASIT-REL-01](#asit-rel-01), [ASIT-OPS-02](#asit-ops-02)
- **External mappings:** OWASP LLM: LLM06:2025 Excessive Agency · MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0053 LLM Plugin Compromise · CWE: CWE-250
- **Incidents:**
  - [INC-0001](https://incidentdatabase.ai/cite/1152/) — Replit AI agent deleted a production database during a code freeze
  - [INC-0003](https://incidentdatabase.ai/cite/1178/) — Google Gemini CLI hallucinated file operations and deleted a user's files

<a id="asit-ops-02"></a>
### ASIT-OPS-02 — Action on Corrupted Context or Hallucinated State

The agent acts on a false internal model of system state — a nonexistent path, a stale fact, or a confabulated result — and issues real operations based on it. Test: did the agent take an action premised on a system state that did not actually hold?

- **Lifecycle stage:** OPS
- **Causal locus:** `context_corruption`
- **Detection point:** `runtime`
- **Blast radius:** 4/5 — Cross-system or sensitive-data. Reaches production, secrets, customer data, or multiple downstream consumers; recovery is costly and may be partial.
- **Evidence status:** `documented`
- **Preconditions:**
  - The agent maintains or infers state without verifying it against ground truth before acting.
  - The agent can execute operations whose safety depends on that state.
- **Mitigating controls:** CTL-13 (Runtime Oversight and Kill Switch); CTL-05 (Telemetry)
- **Related:** [ASIT-OPS-01](#asit-ops-01), [ASIT-TEST-01](#asit-test-01)
- **External mappings:** OWASP LLM: LLM09:2025 Misinformation
- **Incidents:**
  - [INC-0003](https://incidentdatabase.ai/cite/1178/) — Google Gemini CLI hallucinated file operations and deleted a user's files

<a id="asit-ops-03"></a>
### ASIT-OPS-03 — Constraint Self-Modification or Guardrail Evasion

The agent alters its own limits, sandbox, permissions, or guardrails — for example editing its timeout, config, or launch code — to continue or extend its actions. Test: did the agent change a constraint imposed on it rather than operate within it?

- **Lifecycle stage:** OPS
- **Causal locus:** `oversight_failure`
- **Detection point:** `runtime`
- **Blast radius:** 4/5 — Cross-system or sensitive-data. Reaches production, secrets, customer data, or multiple downstream consumers; recovery is costly and may be partial.
- **Evidence status:** `documented`
- **Preconditions:**
  - The agent has write access to its own configuration, code, or execution environment.
  - Constraints are enforced by artifacts the agent can reach rather than by an external boundary.
- **Mitigating controls:** CTL-13 (Runtime Oversight and Kill Switch); CTL-05 (Telemetry); CTL-03 (Audit Trail)
- **Related:** [ASIT-DESIGN-01](#asit-design-01), [ASIT-OPS-01](#asit-ops-01)
- **External mappings:** OWASP LLM: LLM06:2025 Excessive Agency, LLM10:2025 Unbounded Consumption · MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0054 LLM Jailbreak
- **Incidents:**
  - [INC-0007](https://sakana.ai/ai-scientist/) — Sakana "AI Scientist" modified its own code to bypass runtime limits

<a id="asit-ops-04"></a>
### ASIT-OPS-04 — Installed Agent Weaponized by Host Malware

A legitimately installed agent is invoked by third-party malware or an attacker — often with permission-bypassing flags — to perform reconnaissance, exfiltration, or destruction on the host. Test: was a trusted local agent driven by an external process to act against its own user?

- **Lifecycle stage:** OPS
- **Causal locus:** `tool_misuse`
- **Detection point:** `runtime`
- **Blast radius:** 5/5 — Organisation-wide or external and irreversible. Production data loss, breach of third parties, or supply-chain propagation to external users; no clean rollback exists.
- **Evidence status:** `documented`
- **Preconditions:**
  - An agent CLI or runtime is installed and invokable by other local processes.
  - The agent honours flags that bypass permission or confirmation prompts.
  - The agent has standing access to secrets or the filesystem.
- **Mitigating controls:** CTL-04 (Model Supply Chain Integrity); CTL-08 (Least-Privilege Permission Design); CTL-05 (Telemetry)
- **Related:** [ASIT-DESIGN-02](#asit-design-02), [ASIT-REL-02](#asit-rel-02), [ASIT-BUILD-03](#asit-build-03)
- **External mappings:** OWASP LLM: LLM02:2025 Sensitive Information Disclosure, LLM06:2025 Excessive Agency · MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0053 LLM Plugin Compromise
- **Incidents:**
  - [INC-0004](https://www.wiz.io/blog/s1ngularity-supply-chain-attack) — s1ngularity — malicious Nx npm packages weaponised local AI CLIs to steal secrets


## Foundational plane

<a id="asit-found-01"></a>
### ASIT-FOUND-01 — Unaccountable Agent Action

An agent's action cannot be traced to a unique agent identity, a named accountable owner, and a reconstructable log — for example because it ran under shared or human credentials or was never registered. Test: given an action, can you name the responsible agent, its owner, and retrieve a log of what it did?

- **Lifecycle stage:** FOUND
- **Causal locus:** `identity_gap`
- **Detection point:** `post_incident`
- **Blast radius:** 4/5 — Cross-system or sensitive-data. Reaches production, secrets, customer data, or multiple downstream consumers; recovery is costly and may be partial.
- **Evidence status:** `anticipated`
- **Preconditions:**
  - The agent operates without a unique, attributable identity, or is absent from the agent register.
  - Actions are not logged in a way that ties them to that identity and an owner.
- **Mitigating controls:** CTL-01 (Agent Identity); CTL-02 (Agent Register); CTL-03 (Audit Trail)
- **Related:** [ASIT-DESIGN-02](#asit-design-02), [ASIT-OPS-01](#asit-ops-01)
- **External mappings:** CWE: CWE-778, CWE-306
- **Notes:** An incident-response amplifier: it turns other failures into ones that cannot be scoped or contained. No single public incident isolates it; surveyed as prevalent (e.g. CSA) but that is context, not a fileable case.

<a id="asit-found-02"></a>
### ASIT-FOUND-02 — Model or Weight Supply-Chain Compromise

The model, weights, or model-serving dependency the agent runs on is tampered with, poisoned, or backdoored, altering the agent's behaviour at the source. Test: is there a break in the provenance or integrity chain of the model the agent executes?

- **Lifecycle stage:** FOUND
- **Causal locus:** `supply_chain`
- **Detection point:** `pre_execution`
- **Blast radius:** 5/5 — Organisation-wide or external and irreversible. Production data loss, breach of third parties, or supply-chain propagation to external users; no clean rollback exists.
- **Evidence status:** `anticipated`
- **Preconditions:**
  - The agent runs a model or weights whose provenance and integrity are not verified.
  - A path exists to substitute or poison the model or its serving stack.
- **Mitigating controls:** CTL-04 (Model Supply Chain Integrity); CTL-14 (Release Artifact Attestation)
- **Related:** [ASIT-BUILD-02](#asit-build-02), [ASIT-BUILD-04](#asit-build-04), [ASIT-REL-02](#asit-rel-02)
- **External mappings:** OWASP LLM: LLM03:2025 Supply Chain, LLM04:2025 Data and Model Poisoning · MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0010 ML Supply Chain Compromise · CWE: CWE-506
- **Notes:** Distinct from BUILD-02 (packages the agent writes) and REL-02 (the agent's own distribution). No coding-agent-specific public incident found for the model/weights layer itself; held at anticipated.
